mqa_new.label: New MQA Accounts This Week
mqa_new.description: A Marketing Qualified Account (MQA) is an account that crossed a significant engagement threshold through marketing activity. An account reaches MQA when it either: (1) accumulates 200+ marketing engagement points from campaigns, form fills, and visits to key pages in the last 3 months, or (2) has 2+ senior contacts (Director, VP, C-suite) each with 30+ marketing engagement points. These are all accounts that became marketing-qualified in the last 7 days.

hvp.label: Accounts Visiting High-Value Pages (Lost Opp in Last 12 Mo.)
hvp.description: Accounts that had a Closed Lost opportunity in the last 12 months but are back visiting Shopify Plus pages this week. These are re-engagement opportunities — the timing is right to reopen the conversation.

hvp_all.label: Accounts Visiting High-Value Pages
hvp_all.description: All accounts visiting Shopify Plus and enterprise pages this week — not just lost opps. This is the full picture of who is browsing high-value content right now, regardless of deal history.

new_people.label: Newly Engaged People This Week
new_people.description: Brand-new contacts from target buying-committee titles (marketing, ecommerce, digital, C-suite) who engaged with Shopify for the first time ever this week. Fresh inbound signals from people who weren't previously in our orbit.

activity.label: Newly Engaged People — Activity Details
activity.description: The specific activity that drove engagement from each newly engaged person. Engagement types include: email opens and clicks, form fills, webinar registrations and attendance, event sign-ups, content downloads, and direct visits to key pages. Each row shows the person, the type of activity (e.g. 'Attended webinar', 'Filled out contact form'), and the details of what they interacted with. Use this to personalize your outreach — a form fill is a much stronger signal than an email open.

all_mqa.label: All Accounts at MQA Status
all_mqa.description: A snapshot of every account currently at MQA status in your book. This is your full MQA universe — not just what moved this week, but every account that has qualified and is still active. Use this as a reference to prioritize across your pipeline.

intent_agentic.label: Agentic Commerce Intent
intent_agentic.description: Accounts in your book that Demandbase has detected researching AI and agentic commerce topics across the web — not just on Shopify.com. The 'Signals' column shows the exact keywords they triggered (e.g. 'ai chatbot', 'large language models'). The 'Engagement (3mo)' number is their total Demandbase engagement score over the last 3 months — it's a composite of every tracked interaction: page visits, content downloads, ad clicks, and intent keyword matches. Higher = more active. 500+ is strong; 1,000+ is very strong. Use the signals column to tailor your outreach — 'I saw you're researching AI in commerce' opens a better conversation than a cold intro.

intent_compete.label: Competitive Intent
intent_compete.description: Accounts that Demandbase has detected researching Shopify's competitors across the web — reading reviews, visiting competitor sites, searching for comparisons. The 'Researching' column shows which platforms they're looking at (e.g. 'salesforce commerce cloud', 'bigcommerce'). This is an active evaluation signal — they are in-market right now. The 'Engagement (3mo)' number is their overall Demandbase engagement score (web activity + content + intent keywords) over the last 3 months. Accounts here are prime for a Shopify comparison conversation.

intent_international.label: International Commerce Intent
intent_international.description: Accounts showing intent around cross-border commerce, international expansion, and global selling. Shopify Markets is the angle.

intent_marketing.label: Marketing & Growth Intent
intent_marketing.description: Accounts researching marketing, growth, and commerce platform topics aligned to Shopify's marketing and retail offerings.

intent_b2b.label: B2B Commerce Intent
intent_b2b.description: Accounts researching B2B commerce topics — POS, ERP integration, credit management. Shopify B2B and POS are the angles.

top_leads.label: Sales Nav Top Leads
top_leads.description: Your highest-priority contacts from LinkedIn Sales Navigator, ranked by account fit quality. Leads at accounts with active Demandbase intent signals this week are highlighted — these are the people to reach out to right now.

garmin international.merchant_overview: Kansas. That’s where Garmin put a stake in the ground in 1989. We’ve grown substantially over the years, offering diverse products and global reach in 5 diverse markets. But some things won’t ever change: Our entrepreneurial spirit and a culture where everyone’s opinion matters. That’s because we believe it’s possible to light the tech world on fire and still be on a first name basis. BIG IDEAS, SMALL EGOS What we do best is make products that let our customers do what they love. To do that, we focus on practical innovation, never losing sight of the benefits our products bring to our customers. Sure, we love bells and whistles. What leading technology company doesn't? But we make sure ours have purpose. It’s not about the things we make. It’s about the things we make possible. WE’RE GLOBAL, SERIOUSLY. Yeah, we’re based in Kansas City, but we are truly a global company. With offices throughout Europe, Asia, Middle East, South America and Australia, our products are sold all over the world. One elevator ride at our headquarters and you’re likely to hear 4 (or more) different languages being spoken. At Garmin, we may act local, but we think global. DREAM BIG. SLEEP WELL. At Garmin, we like to encourage dreamers. But at some point it’s nice to wake up and enjoy a fully aware life. So we make it super easy to achieve a good balance between working, dreaming and enjoying life—from workout classes offered throughout the day, breaks to get out and move on our campus, and wellness programs offered throughout the year. It’s almost impossible not to take advantage of Garmin’s work-life balance opportunities.

philips electronics nederland b.v..merchant_overview: Philips Electronics Nederland B.V. is part of Royal Philips, a leading global health technology company headquartered in the Netherlands. While Philips Electronics Nederland B.V. is a legal entity focused on Philips’ operations in the Netherlands, its business model aligns with the broader Royal Philips group, which is organized around health technology innovation and commercialization. Business Model of Philips Electronics Nederland B.V. Health Technology Focus: The company is centered on improving people’s health and well-being, delivering integrated solutions across the health continuum—from healthy living and prevention to diagnosis, treatment, and home care 3 5 . Core business areas include diagnostic imaging, image-guided therapy, patient monitoring, health informatics, and consumer health/home care 3 5 . Innovation and R&D: Philips invests heavily in research and development, with over 11,000 employees (over 13% of the workforce) dedicated to R&D and €1.9 billion invested in R&D in 2020 (about 10% of sales at the group level) 3 . The company engages in open innovation, collaborating with academic and industrial partners, as well as through European and regional projects, to share risks and accelerate development 3 . Global Reach and Operations: Philips operates in more than 100 countries, with sales and service delivered through a mix of direct sales, distributors, and business partners 3 5 . The company has shifted to a more decentralized structure, with most business units based abroad, but maintains its headquarters and significant activities in the Netherlands 6 . Sustainability and Circularity: Philips is committed to sustainable business practices, aiming for 100% of its products and services to be designed in line with EcoDesign requirements by 2025, and for 25% of sales to come from circular products and solutions 1 . The company has set targets for renewable energy use, waste reduction, and water consumption 1 . Supply Chain and Tariff Mitigation: Philips sources products globally and is impacted by international trade policies. For 2025, the company anticipates a net impact from tariffs of €250–300 million, despite efforts to mitigate costs through pricing adjustments and supply chain localization 7 8 . Revenue Figures and Online/Offline Sales Revenue Figures: Philips Electronics Nederland B.V. itself does not publish separate revenue figures. As a subsidiary, its financials are consolidated within Royal Philips. Royal Philips Group Revenue: In recent years, group sales have been reported at around €17–18 billion annually (e.g., €17.3 billion in 2021) 3 . Q1 2025 Performance: The group’s latest available figures show ongoing operations and sustainability progress, but do not break out Dutch or subsidiary-level revenue 1 2 . Online vs. Offline Sales: Sales Model: Philips primarily sells its products and solutions through a B2B model (hospitals, healthcare providers, distributors) and B2C model (consumer health products via retaile
